J 
December 27, 1989 
Daniel E. stone 
Rossmoor Community 
District 
3232 Hedwig Road 
Rossmoor, CA 
90720 
Re: 
Your 
for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-670 
Dear Hr. stone: 
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on 
behalf of Rossmoor Community Services District Board Hember 
Gustave Brickman regarding the mass mailing provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act,, ).1 
Because your request is more 
of a general inquiry than a request for advice as to a specific 
action pending before the board, we treat your request as one for 
informal assistance. 2 
QUESTION 
Under what circumstances, may board members suggest items to 
be printed in the district's newsletter? 
CONCLUSION 
The mass mailing provisions of the Act do not prevent board 
members from suggesting items to be printed in the district's 
newsletter. 
If board members suggest items to be printed in the 
newsletter, the newsletter will be deemed to be prepared in co-
operation, consultation, coordination or concert with the board 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commiss 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2

does not provide the requestor with the

You are the general manager of the Rossmoor Commun 
Services Di 
ct. 
One of the board members, Gustave Brickman, 
wants to know if board members can suggest items, at an open board 
meeting or otherwise, to be printed in the district's newsletter. 
The suggestions would relate to items about which the public 
should be informed or items of interest to residents of the com-
munity. 
The items suggested would have nothing to do with 
individual board members. 
For example, one item might concern the 
problem of vehicles parking on the street on street sweeping days. 
on 8 001 
Act 
that " no newsletter or 
other mass 
il 
shall be sent at publ 
expense. II 
A mass mail-
ing is def 
as two hundred or more substantially similar p 
of mail. 
(Section 82041.5.) 
lation 18901 clarif 
that the 
purpose of section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from us 
public funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings at 
public expense. 
We have previously advised your district that a mass mailing 
may not be sent at public expense by a community services district 
if it contains the name, office, photograph or any other reference 
to an elected officer of the district and is prepared or sent in 
ion, consultation, coordination or concert with the 
elected off 
(Regulation l890l(c) (2); Aleshire Advice Letter, 
No. I-89-539, copies enclosed.)3 
We believe that suggestions from board members as to the 
contents of the district's newsletter result in the newsletter 
being prepared in cooperation, consultation, coordination or 
concert with the board members. 
Therefore, the newsletter may not 
contain the name, office, photograph or any other reference to the 
board members, except as otherwise permitted by the regulation. 
For example, the names of the board members may be listed in a 
roster listing of the district officials in which all are listed 
in the same typeface, type size and location in the layout of the 
newsletter. (Regulation l890l~(e); Krannawitter Advice Letter, 
No. A-89-300, copy enclosed.) 
At its meeting of December 13, 1989, the Commission adopted a 
new version of Regulation 18901. 
A copy of the revised regulat 
is enclosed for your review. 
We are not yet advising pursuant to 
the new regulation. 
In this instance, our advice would not change 
under the 
vers

and sent at public expense. 
It mere 
limits references to the elected officer. 
I trust that this letter clarifies the application of the 
mass mailing provisions of the Act with respect to your question. 
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-
5901.

Enclosures

General Counsel 
By: 
W. Ellison 
Counsel, Legal Division 


Novenmer 15, 1989 
Gregory W. Baugher 
Executive Director 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Mr. Baugher, 
Rossmoor Community Services District Board Member Gustave Bricbnan wants to 
know if board members can suggest items, at an open Board Meeting or other-
wise, to be printed in the District's Newsletter if the items are things 
that the District should be informing the public about or if the items are 
of interest to residents of the community and have nothing to do with in-
dividual board menillers. 
For instance, printing an item about a problem in 
the District of vehicles parking on the street on the District's street 
sweeping days. 
I look 
General Manager 
cc; 
Rossmcor ComiTiunity Services District Board 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November 20, 1989 
Daniel E. stone 
General Manager, Rossmoor Community 
Services District 
3232 Hedwig Road 
Rossmoor, CA 
90720 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-670 
Dear Mr. Stone: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on November 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,
